name: Predicting U.S. Unemployment Using Macroeconomic Indicators
description: A quarterly time series dataset integrating U.S. GDP growth rate, unemployment rate, and Consumer Price Index for commodities from 1956 to 2025. Built to support predictive modeling of U.S. unemployment using macroeconomic indicators. Datasets were sourced from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database, cleaned, aggregated to quarterly frequency, and merged on observation date.

variableMeasured.name: gdp_growth_rate
variableMeasured.description: Quarterly percent change in real U.S. GDP, seasonally adjusted at annual rates

variableMeasured.name: unemployment_rate
variableMeasured.description: Seasonally adjusted U.S. unemployment rate, averaged to quarterly frequency

variableMeasured.name: cpi_commodities
variableMeasured.description: Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers: Commodities, averaged to quarterly frequency. Baseline 1982-1984 = 100.

includedInDataCatalog.name: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED

measurementTechnique: Data was downloaded from FRED as CSV files, aggregated from monthly to quarterly frequency using averaging, merged on observation date using an inner join, and processed using Python and pandas.
